edges.description: Merlin loss releases Hippo pathway control of YAP1.
YAP1 activation promotes proliferative and migratory phenotypes in meningioma cells.
NOTCH3-high states correlate with FAO-related metabolic features.
Enhanced FAO-linked metabolism is associated with ferroptosis evasion.
Higher NY-ESO-1 expression increases susceptibility to NY-ESO-1-directed TCR-T killing.
edges.source: NY-ESO-1-Associated Immune Vulnerability in Malignant Meningioma

NY-ESO-1-Associated Immune Vulnerability in Malignant Meningioma

meta.association: Recurrent activating mutation in non-NF2 meningioma

evidence.reference_title: Genomic analysis of non-NF2 meningiomas reveals mutations in TRAF7, KLF4, AKT1, and SMO.
evidence.snippet: The known neoplasia-related recurrent mutation, AKT1E17K, was identified in 38 meningiomas.

evidence.reference_title: NOTCH3 drives fatty acid oxidation and ferroptosis resistance in aggressive meningiomas.
evidence.snippet: Single-cell RNA sequencing revealed a correlation with CD36, a key fatty acid transporter.

nodes.description: NOTCH3-associated meningioma states show increased CD36-linked fatty acid utilization and FAO-like mitochondrial metabolism.

evidence.reference_title: NOTCH3 drives fatty acid oxidation and ferroptosis resistance in aggressive meningiomas.
evidence.snippet: Single-cell RNA sequencing revealed a correlation with CD36, a key fatty acid transporter.

evidence.reference_title: NOTCH3 drives fatty acid oxidation and ferroptosis resistance in aggressive meningiomas.
evidence.snippet: NOTCH3 ICD overexpression (OE) exhibited depletion of fatty acid pools, alongside transcriptional upregulation of canonical FAO genes.

meta.association: Homozygous or heterozygous deletion associated with progression

evidence.reference_title: CDKN2A/B deletions are strongly associated with meningioma progression: a meta-analysis of individual patient data
evidence.snippet: Both hetero- or homozygous CDKN2A/B deletions were significantly associated with shortened time to meningioma progression.

nodes.description: CDKN2A and CDKN2B deletion marks aggressive meningioma biology and is linked to shorter progression-free survival, with homozygous deletion incorporated into modern high-grade risk assessment.

evidence.reference_title: CDKN2A/B deletions are strongly associated with meningioma progression: a meta-analysis of individual patient data
evidence.snippet: Both hetero- or homozygous CDKN2A/B deletions were significantly associated with shortened time to meningioma progression.

evidence.reference_title: Epigenetic, Genetic, and Transcriptomic Profiling of Meningiomas: Molecular Patterns Correlate with Clinical Prognosis
evidence.snippet: The grading system established by the World Health Organization (WHO) has recently included pTERT mutations and CDKN2A/B homozygous deletions as criteria for grade 3, owing to their association with increased recurrence risk.

meta.association: Homozygous or heterozygous deletion associated with progression

evidence.reference_title: CDKN2A/B deletions are strongly associated with meningioma progression: a meta-analysis of individual patient data
evidence.snippet: Both hetero- or homozygous CDKN2A/B deletions were significantly associated with shortened time to meningioma progression.

meta.association: Higher expression in higher-grade malignant meningioma

evidence.reference_title: Immunotherapeutic targeting of NY-ESO-1 in malignant meningiomas with TCR-transduced T-cells.
evidence.snippet: NY-ESO-1 expression correlated with tumor grade (n = 35; p < 0.01).

nodes.description: YAP1 activation promotes meningioma-cell proliferation, migration, and anchorage-independent growth downstream of Hippo pathway deregulation.

evidence.reference_title: Yes-Associated Protein 1 Is Activated and Functions as an Oncogene in Meningiomas
evidence.snippet: Using an siRNA transient knockdown of YAP1 in NF2-mutant meningioma cells, we show that suppression of YAP1 impaired cell proliferation and migration.

evidence.reference_title: Yes-Associated Protein 1 Is Activated and Functions as an Oncogene in Meningiomas
evidence.snippet: Conversely, YAP1 overexpression led to a strong augment of cell proliferation and anchorage-independent growth and restriction of cisplatin-induced apoptosis.

nodes.description: Aggressive meningioma cells with activated NOTCH3 signaling display reduced sensitivity to ferroptosis, indicating a potential mechanism of treatment resistance.

evidence.reference_title: NOTCH3 drives fatty acid oxidation and ferroptosis resistance in aggressive meningiomas.
evidence.snippet: Additionally, NOTCH3 OE cells exhibit increased resistance to RSL3-induced ferroptosis, a phenotype that was reversed with CPT1 inhibition.

nodes.description: Merlin loss disrupts Hippo pathway control and permits YAP1 activation, promoting meningioma cell proliferation, migration, and tumorigenic behavior.

evidence.reference_title: Yes-Associated Protein 1 Is Activated and Functions as an Oncogene in Meningiomas
evidence.snippet: Here, we show that primary meningioma tumors have high nuclear expression of YAP1.

evidence.reference_title: Yes-Associated Protein 1 Is Activated and Functions as an Oncogene in Meningiomas
evidence.snippet: Together, these findings suggest that in meningiomas, deregulation of the Hippo pathway is largely observed in primary tumors and that YAP1 functions as an oncogene promoting meningioma tumorigenesis.

evidence.reference_title: Genomic analysis of non-NF2 meningiomas reveals mutations in TRAF7, KLF4, AKT1, and SMO.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in TRAF7 commonly occurred with a recurrent mutation (K409Q) in KLF4, a transcription factor known for its role in inducing pluripotency, or with AKT1(E17K), a mutation known to activate the PI3K pathway.

evidence.reference_title: Yes-Associated Protein 1 Is Activated and Functions as an Oncogene in Meningiomas
evidence.snippet: Loss of the NF2 tumor suppressor gene is the most common genetic alteration in meningiomas, and the NF2 gene product, Merlin, acts upstream of the Hippo pathway.

nodes.description: NF2 loss inactivates the Merlin tumor suppressor, one of the central early genetic events in meningioma tumorigenesis and a major axis separating NF2 altered from non-NF2 molecular groups.

evidence.reference_title: Yes-Associated Protein 1 Is Activated and Functions as an Oncogene in Meningiomas
evidence.snippet: Loss of the NF2 tumor suppressor gene is the most common genetic alteration in meningiomas, and the NF2 gene product, Merlin, acts upstream of the Hippo pathway.

evidence.reference_title: Epigenetic, Genetic, and Transcriptomic Profiling of Meningiomas: Molecular Patterns Correlate with Clinical Prognosis
evidence.snippet: Meningiomas in the second group have an intermediate prognosis and are characterized by NF2 alterations, mild chromosomal instability, and enrichment in immune cells.

meta.association: Upregulated signaling in aggressive meningioma models

evidence.reference_title: NOTCH3 drives fatty acid oxidation and ferroptosis resistance in aggressive meningiomas.
evidence.snippet: While prior work has linked NOTCH3 expression to higher-grade meningiomas and treatment resistance, the metabolic phenotype of NOTCH3 activation remains unexplored in meningioma.

nodes.description: NOTCH3 signaling is associated with aggressive meningioma behavior and links to downstream metabolic reprogramming.

evidence.reference_title: NOTCH3 drives fatty acid oxidation and ferroptosis resistance in aggressive meningiomas.
evidence.snippet: While prior work has linked NOTCH3 expression to higher-grade meningiomas and treatment resistance, the metabolic phenotype of NOTCH3 activation remains unexplored in meningioma.

evidence.reference_title: NOTCH3 drives fatty acid oxidation and ferroptosis resistance in aggressive meningiomas.
evidence.snippet: We show that NOTCH3 mediates a metabolic shift towards fatty acid oxidation (FAO), depleting lipid availability and conferring resistance to ferroptosis.

nodes.description: Preclinical adoptive cell transfer using NY-ESO-1-directed TCR-T cells shows anti-tumor activity and survival benefit in high-grade meningioma models.

evidence.reference_title: Immunotherapeutic targeting of NY-ESO-1 in malignant meningiomas with TCR-transduced T-cells.
evidence.snippet: Systemic ACT results in significantly increased survival in vivo in high-grade meningioma.

nodes.description: Higher NY-ESO-1 expression in malignant meningioma is linked to higher tumor grade and may expose a targetable immune vulnerability.
nodes.id: NY-ESO-1-Associated Immune Vulnerability in Malignant Meningioma

evidence.reference_title: Immunotherapeutic targeting of NY-ESO-1 in malignant meningiomas with TCR-transduced T-cells.
evidence.snippet: NY-ESO-1 expression correlated with tumor grade (n = 35; p < 0.01).

nodes.description: Meningioma cells with higher NY-ESO-1 expression show stronger cytolytic response to NY-ESO-1-targeted TCR-transduced T cells.

evidence.reference_title: Immunotherapeutic targeting of NY-ESO-1 in malignant meningiomas with TCR-transduced T-cells.
evidence.snippet: CH157-HLA-A2.1 cells, with native high NY-ESO-1 expression, experienced > 60% and then nearly 100% cytolysis after co-culture with TCR-T for 10 and 24 h, respectively, compared with control T-cells (p < 0.0001).

evidence.reference_title: Immunotherapeutic targeting of NY-ESO-1 in malignant meningiomas with TCR-transduced T-cells.
evidence.snippet: Systemic ACT results in significantly increased survival in vivo in high-grade meningioma.

nodes.description: A substantial subset of meningiomas lacks NF2 alteration and instead carries mutually exclusive driver mutations in TRAF7, KLF4, AKT1, SMO, and related signaling genes, producing clinically and anatomically distinct molecular subgroups.

evidence.reference_title: Genomic analysis of non-NF2 meningiomas reveals mutations in TRAF7, KLF4, AKT1, and SMO.
evidence.snippet: Besides NF2, we identified increased mutation burden in TNF receptor-associated factor 7 (TRAF7), Krupple-like factor 4 (KLF4), v-akt murine thymoma viral oncogene homolog 1 (AKT1), and Smoothened, frizzled family receptor (SMO) (as a group, referred to as non-NF2 mutant hereafter) (Fig. 1).

evidence.reference_title: Genomic analysis of non-NF2 meningiomas reveals mutations in TRAF7, KLF4, AKT1, and SMO.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in these genes were mutually exclusive of NF2 mutations.

evidence.reference_title: Genomic sequencing of meningiomas identifies oncogenic SMO and AKT1 mutations.
evidence.snippet: A subset of meningiomas lacking NF2 alterations harbored recurrent oncogenic mutations in AKT1 (p.Glu17Lys) and SMO (p.Trp535Leu) and exhibited immunohistochemical evidence of activation of these pathways.

evidence.reference_title: Recurrent somatic mutations in POLR2A define a distinct subset of meningiomas
evidence.snippet: Recurrent somatic mutations in POLR2A define a distinct subset of meningiomas

evidence.reference_title: Genomic analysis of non-NF2 meningiomas reveals mutations in TRAF7, KLF4, AKT1, and SMO.
evidence.snippet: In addition, we identified single mutations in genes previously reported to play a role in other neoplasias, including CREBBP, PIK3CA (R108H variant), PIK3R1 (deletion p.306-307), and BRCA1 as well as two SMARCB1 mutations, which coexisted with NF2 loss and have previously been reported in meningiomas (4) (table S3).

meta.association: Recurrent somatic mutation defining a distinct non-NF2 subset

evidence.reference_title: Recurrent somatic mutations in POLR2A define a distinct subset of meningiomas
evidence.snippet: Recurrent somatic mutations in POLR2A define a distinct subset of meningiomas

evidence.reference_title: Genomic analysis of non-NF2 meningiomas reveals mutations in TRAF7, KLF4, AKT1, and SMO.
evidence.snippet: Finally, in 11 tumors, we identified mutations in SMO, which is expressed in meningiomas (fig. S5).

meta.association: Promoter mutation associated with WHO grade 3 biology

evidence.reference_title: Epigenetic, Genetic, and Transcriptomic Profiling of Meningiomas: Molecular Patterns Correlate with Clinical Prognosis
evidence.snippet: The grading system established by the World Health Organization (WHO) has recently included pTERT mutations and CDKN2A/B homozygous deletions as criteria for grade 3, owing to their association with increased recurrence risk.

nodes.description: TERT promoter mutations identify a molecularly high-risk meningioma subset associated with recurrence risk and WHO grade 3 assignment in modern classification.

evidence.reference_title: Epigenetic, Genetic, and Transcriptomic Profiling of Meningiomas: Molecular Patterns Correlate with Clinical Prognosis
evidence.snippet: The grading system established by the World Health Organization (WHO) has recently included pTERT mutations and CDKN2A/B homozygous deletions as criteria for grade 3, owing to their association with increased recurrence risk.
